trimNote: Sanitized the recording scratch-directory absolute path down to station-work/ — noise removal, not content. The full folder listing and full 12-row CSV dump were shortened to one-line summaries since the ranked table in the final turn already restates every track.

turns.text: There is one spreadsheet in this folder — candidates for Tuesday's adds meeting. Read it, then give me a ranked list for the meeting: which tracks should we talk about first. Flag every local artist clearly, since those get priority in our rotation policy. Don't recommend actually adding anything — that's a human call in the meeting — just help me walk in prepared.
I'll read the spreadsheet and put together a ranked prep list for you.

tool.name: Read
tool.result: 12 rows, 8 columns — artist, track, label, release_date, local, genre, spins_last_week, notes.

turns.text:
Read it — 12 candidate tracks, 6 local and 6 not. Here's a ranked talking order, plus two data problems you should know about before you walk in.

## One caution first

The file is named `music-adds-SYNTHETIC.csv`, and the contents look like test data, not a real export. The **genre column is clearly unreliable**: Abby Jeanne is tagged hip-hop, Nickel&Rose is tagged electronic, and five of the six non-local tracks are tagged hip-hop even though they're on Sub Pop, 4AD, Merge, Dead Oceans, Anti-, and Matador. I ranked on things I can actually trust from the sheet — local status, release date, and spins — and ignored genre entirely. If this is meant to be the real candidate list, someone should re-pull it.

## Ranked talking order

Locals first, per your rotation policy. Today is Sunday 7/12; I treated the meeting as Tuesday 7/14.

| # | Track | Local? | Why it's here |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Abby Jeanne — "Neon Hymn"** | **LOCAL** | Out since 7/8 with 12 spins — the strongest local traction on the sheet, and a decision that's already a few days late. |
| 2 | **Klassik — "Milwaukee Sun"** | **LOCAL** | Drops the day of the meeting. 0 spins simply because it isn't out yet. If it isn't decided Tuesday, you miss release week. |
| 3 | **B~Free — "Sideways"** | **LOCAL** | Also drops 7/14. Same release-week timing pressure, less existing traction (3 spins). |
| 4 | **Lex Allen — "Open Wide"** | **LOCAL** | Out 7/17. Already at 8 spins, so there's appetite; deciding Tuesday means you're ready for release day. |
| 5 | **Immortal Girlfriend — "Cold Static"** | **LOCAL** | Out 7/21. Real candidate, but it could survive being held to next week's meeting if you run out of time. |
| 6 | **Nickel&Rose — "Half Light"** | **LOCAL** | Out since 7/11, only 1 spin. Weakest signal of the locals — but it's local, so policy puts it above the national list. |
| 7 | Static Palace — "Overpass" (Merge) | no | 14 spins, the highest number in the room, and out since 7/2. You're effectively already playing it; this is a "formalize it or stop" conversation. |
| 8 | Nightjar — "Tin Roof" (Dead Oceans) | no | 10 spins, releases 7/19. Second-most traction. |
| 9 | The Vanishing — "Paper Radio" (Sub Pop) | no | 7 spins, just came out 7/12. |
| 10 | Wide Ocean — "Signal Fade" (Matador) | no | 4 spins, out 7/15. |
| 11 | Marigold Fields — "Slow Bloom" (4AD) | no | 3 spins, out since 7/6 — a week out with little movement. |
| 12 | Ruthie Blue — "Anyhow" (Anti-) | no | Out 7/12, 0 spins. Least evidence of anything; save it for last. |

## The one thing I'd raise in the room

If you only get through half the list, the top four are the ones that actually expire: Klassik and B~Free both drop Tuesday, Lex Allen drops Friday, and Abby Jeanne is already out. Items 5 through 12 will all still be live decisions next week.

Also worth asking someone: **what does `spins_last_week` actually count?** Several tracks have spins before their release date — Nightjar has 10 spins and doesn't come out until 7/19. That's normal if it's counting specialty-show or advance plays, but if you're reading those numbers as rotation performance, they mean something different than you think.

I've deliberately not said whether anything should be added — that's the room's call.
